mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in greater than 55 percent of months following a federal funds rate decline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, causing investors to discount future earnings and driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in greater than 60 percent of instances when unemployment rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over three months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms robust consumer demand and wealth effects, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter through sustained corporate revenue growth.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 60-day returns in greater than 50 percent of occurrences, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates strengthening manufacturing output and supply chain normalization, which triggers short-term positive S&P 500 returns within one week as market participants price in improved corporate margins.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in greater than 55 percent of weeks following a positive industrial production surprise.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price index growth above 3.5 percent annually forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, increasing discount rates and compressing equity valuations to produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in greater than 65 percent of months when CPIAUCSL exceeds the 3.5 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a resilient labor market that supports consumer spending and corporate profitability, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a rank IC greater than 0.05 over any rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly gross domestic product growth reflects broad economic expansion that directly translates into higher corporate earnings expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in greater than 50 percent of quarters following positive GDP growth readings.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production exceeding 1.0 percent signals immediate supply chain disruptions or demand destruction, triggering rapid risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in greater than 60 percent of weeks following an industrial production drop greater than 1.0 percent.
